channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump faces criticism for not have evacuation plans in place before starting the war
item.description: As Iranian strikes target U.S. facilities across the Middle East, the State Department races to evacuate thousands of Americans caught in a rapidly evolving war zone.
item.content:encoded: As Iranian strikes target U.S. facilities across the Middle East, the State Department  races to evacuate thousands of Americans caught in a rapidly evolving war zone.
item.dc:creator: Michele Kelemen

item.title: Iranian civilians are now fleeing the relentless bombing for neighboring Turkey
item.description: As the U.S. military broadens its strikes in Iran, traumatized Iranians are reaching the border with Turkey.
item.content:encoded: As the U.S. military broadens its strikes in Iran, traumatized Iranians are reaching the border with Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Sherlock

item.title: As Trumps plans visit to China, arms package to Taiwan is delayed
item.description: Two American arms package sales to Taiwan have been delayed as President Trump prepares to visit China, raising questions about the United States' commitment to Taiwan.
item.content:encoded: Two American arms package sales to Taiwan have been delayed as President Trump prepares to visit China, raising questions about the United States' commitment to Taiwan.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Cuban Americans in South Florida are preparing for possible regime change in Cuba
item.description: Some Cuban-Americans in South Florida are eager for regime change in Cuba and hope the Trump administration will make it happen.
item.content:encoded: Some Cuban-Americans in South Florida are eager for regime change in Cuba and hope the Trump administration will make it happen.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Rivero

item.title: Six months after Gen Z–led unrest, Nepal votes in its first post-protest election
item.description: Six months after massive youth-led protests that ousted a prime minister, voters in Nepal are headed to the polls on Thursday.
item.content:encoded: Six months after massive youth-led protests that ousted a prime minister, voters in Nepal are headed to the polls on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Preiss

item.title: U.S. submarine sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean as conflict widens
item.description: The U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has expanded to the Indian Ocean, as a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in international waters.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israel's war with Iran has expanded to the Indian Ocean, as a U.S. submarine sank an Iranian warship in international waters.
item.dc:creator: Diaa Hadid

item.title: Tokyo court orders Unification Church's Japan branch to dissolve after fraud ruling
item.description: A court in Japan has ordered the Unification Church's local arm dissolved, after it bilked followers for decades. Ties between the church and Japan's ruling party became a political scandal.
item.content:encoded: A court in Japan has ordered the Unification Church's local arm dissolved, after it bilked followers for decades. Ties between the church and Japan's ruling party became a political scandal.
item.dc:creator: Anthony Kuhn

item.title: In Tacoma, a joyful treasure hunt for 'monkeyshines' has been going on for 2 decades
item.description: In Tacoma, Wash., glass artists hide beautiful glass spheres around the city each winter for residents to find. They're called "monkeyshines," because the first ones were stamped with a monkey stamp.
item.content:encoded: In Tacoma, Wash., glass artists hide beautiful glass spheres around the city each winter for residents to find. They're called "monkeyshines," because the first ones were stamped with a monkey stamp.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Gallup

item.title: Senate committee advances Trump's nominee for public lands chief
item.description: President Trump's controversial nominee to lead the nation's largest public lands agency faces an initial confirmation vote Wednesday as Democrats point to his past support to sell federal lands.
item.content:encoded: President Trump's controversial nominee to lead the nation's largest public lands agency faces an initial confirmation vote Wednesday as Democrats point to his past support to sell federal lands.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: The 2026 midterm elections are officially underway. Here's what to watch
item.description: Voters went to the polls Tuesday in Texas, North Carolina and Arkansas. The results could help determine control of Congress and how people feel about Democratic and Republican party leadership.
item.content:encoded: Voters went to the polls Tuesday in Texas, North Carolina and Arkansas. The results could help determine control of Congress and how people feel about Democratic and Republican party leadership.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Fowler
